message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Update both TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md and .team/ profiles in retros">
Correction to my last message: The mini-retro step should cover BOTH types of updates:

1. TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md — for team-wide process improvements
2. .team/ profiles — for individual lessons learned relevant to each person's expertise

The project owner notes that the team has found process improvements since the last time TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md was updated, but those improvements weren't captured. For example, the Step 9 retro may have had observations that should have gone into TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md but didn't.

So the permanent mini-retro step should be: "If the retro identified changes, update TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md and/or .team/ profiles as appropriate. It's OK to skip if there's genuinely nothing to change."

Please make sure the current batch of profile updates AND any outstanding process improvements all get committed together.
</teammate-message>

content.text:
Understood. The TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md update I just made already covers both types -- it says:

> After the discussion, capture learnings:
> - **Team-wide process changes**: update TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md
> - **Individual expertise learnings**: each team member tells the Driver what to add to their `.team/` profile's "Lessons Learned" section. Skip if no new learnings -- only capture genuine insights, not busywork.

That matches exactly what the project owner described. Now waiting for team members to respond to the mini-retro broadcast with their observations and profile update text. Once I have all responses, I'll batch the profile edits, any TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md updates, and the mini-retro step addition into a single commit.
